summary: Cleanup and matching pass for src/melee/it/it_2725.c with many simplifications, prototype/type corrections, data/split fixes, and a small grlib return-type adjustment. The author reported many it_2725 functions improving to 100% fuzzy match and main/melee/it/it_2725 matched_code rising from 33.30% to 52.48%, with no regressions in fully matched functions.
what_changed: Reworked large portions of src/melee/it/it_2725.c, removing many temporary variables and stale matching comments while replacing direct user_data/hsd_obj access with helper macros such as GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ where shown in the diff.

Simplified item JObj traversal by introducing helpers such as jobj_child and get_bone_by_id, then reusing the latter in it_80274F48, it_80274FDC, and it_80275070 instead of duplicating bone lookup loops.

Corrected it_80273F34 from a three-argument form back to two arguments, removing comments that said the third argument was only being used to protect r5 for matching; all visible call sites were updated accordingly.
Changed it_80274658 from void to int and returned dir2, while also using a more precise radians conversion constant 0.017453292f.
Added additional bitfield union views to struct FtCmdState in src/melee/ft/types.h, including x0_b0_8/x0_b0_2/x0_b0_3 and x0_6_26_1/x0_6_26_2.
Changed grLib_801C9E40 return type from s16 to int in both grlib.c and grlib.h, returning stage_info.x708.
Moved rodata range 0x803B85F0-0x803B8600 from melee/it/it_266F.c to melee/it/it_2725.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark several it_803Fxxxx and it_804Dxxxx objects as data:string and adjusted their sizes, including it_803F1F00, it_803F1F0C, it_803F5428, it_803F5440, it_804D5188, it_804D5190, it_804D51D0, it_804D51D8, it_804D51E0, and it_804D51E8.
Changed it_803B85F0 declaration from s32[4] to two s64 externs, it_803B85F0 and it_803B85F8, consistent with the split rodata attribution.
smart_moves: The cleanup removed a misleading fake third parameter from it_80273F34 instead of preserving it solely as a register-protection matching hack; this improves prototype credibility while still increasing matching.

Factoring repeated JObj bone traversal into get_bone_by_id reduced duplicated control flow across multiple functions and helped several functions reach 100% fuzzy match according to the posted ninja change_all output.
Using GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ made code more idiomatic for the project and reduced manual casts from item_gobj->user_data and item_gobj->hsd_obj.

The PR paired code matching work with config/symbol/split corrections, especially moving a rodata tail from it_266F to it_2725 and annotating string data, so the matching improvements were not limited to source formatting.

The author provided manual ninja change_all output when the bot comment was missing, documenting that fully matched functions had no regressions despite some fuzzy-match decreases in partially matched functions.
decomp_lessons: Do not keep extra function parameters only to satisfy a temporary register allocation theory; re-check call sites and callee behavior, then prefer the semantically correct prototype if it still matches.
When several functions duplicate the same JObj child/dynamic-bone-table traversal, extracting an inline/helper can both clarify intent and improve matching by producing consistent codegen.
Small type corrections in shared headers, such as grLib_801C9E40 returning int rather than s16 or adding FtCmdState bitfield aliases, can unlock matches in unrelated-looking item code.

RoData ownership at split boundaries matters: moving 0x803B85F0-0x803B8600 from it_266F to it_2725 also produced a reported matched_data increase for it_266F and a small matched_data gain for it_2725.
String symbol annotations and accurate object sizes in symbols.txt are useful decomp infrastructure, not just metadata; they help explain and stabilize data layout around item modules.
Manual match-report evidence is valuable when automation is absent; include before/after fuzzy_match, matched_code, and matched_data numbers in PR comments.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based placeholder names for unresolved item symbols and functions, such as it_80273F34, it_80274658, it_803B85F0, and it_804D51D0.
Used concise local helper names that describe structure traversal rather than addresses, such as jobj_child and get_bone_by_id.
Continued project macro style GET_ITEM(item_gobj) and GET_JOBJ(item_gobj) for retrieving typed user data and root JObj from item gobjects.
New FtCmdState bitfield names followed existing placeholder bitfield naming style using byte/bit-width encodings such as x0_b0_8 and x0_6_26_2.
Symbol table entries retained address-derived data names while adding data:string annotations where the object was identified as a string.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a suspected r5-preservation workaround in it_80273F34 by deleting the fake third Item* parameter and simplifying the surrounding calls.
Used inline helper extraction for JObj child traversal and bone lookup to alter control flow and reduce duplicated loop patterns while preserving behavior.
Adjusted stack padding in visible functions, including use of u8 _[8] and PAD_STACK changes, indicating local stack-shape tuning remained part of matching.
Changed constants and return types to better match compiler output, including 0.017453292f in it_80274658 and int return type for grLib_801C9E40.
Reclassified rodata and string data in splits.txt and symbols.txt to improve data matching and module attribution.
Added compile warnings suppressions for sign comparison and return type, likely to tolerate in-progress decomp code during the cleanup, though the PR excerpt does not explain the motivation.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only substantive comment was from the author, noting that there was no bot comment and providing ninja change_all output with no regressions in fully matched functions.
Evidence of reviewer intent is weak because review_comments_excerpt is empty and counts show zero review comments and zero reviews.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type it_80273F34 and confirm the two-argument prototype remained stable.
Search for uses of the new FtCmdState bitfield aliases x0_b0_8/x0_b0_2/x0_b0_3 and x0_6_26_1/x0_6_26_2 in fighter command matching work.
Check whether -Wno-return-type and -Wno-sign-compare were later removed or kept globally after this cleanup.
Inspect subsequent it_2725 PRs for replacements of address-based data names like it_803F5428 and it_804D51D0 with semantic names.
Compare later grLib_801C9E40 call sites to verify whether int return type was required for item or stage matching.
